title: You Only Look Once: Unified, Real-Time Object Detection
authors: Joseph Redmon, Santosh Divvala, Ross Girshick, Ali Farhadi
hook: YOLO lets computers find every object in an image in one fast glance.
promise: You will understand how YOLO turns object detection into a single rapid prediction step.
prerequisite_note: If you know images are made of pixels and computers can learn patterns, you are ready.

glossary.plain: Finding and labeling every object in an image with a box and a name.
A rectangle that tightly frames an object in an image.
Dividing an image into smaller squares to help locate objects.
The chance that a detected object belongs to a certain category.
Predicting numbers directly, like box positions, instead of picking from categories.
Training the whole system in one go, so all parts work together.

cards.title: Find every object at once
body: See how YOLO detects everything in an image with one swift prediction.

cards.title: How do computers spot things?
body: Imagine asking a computer to find every dog, car, and person in a photo. Traditionally, it would scan each part of the image separately.
This slow, step-by-step search made real-time object detection nearly impossible. YOLO changes everything by letting the computer look at the whole image at once.

cards.title: From image to boxes in one go
body: YOLO splits the image into a grid. Each grid cell predicts if it contains an object and where the object is.
Instead of checking regions one by one, YOLO's neural network learns to predict all bounding boxes and their classes in one pass.
As a result, detection becomes much faster and more unified, with every object found in a single evaluation.
cards.middle: Neural network predicts boxes and classes
cards.caption: YOLO's grid-based approach enables instant object detection across the whole image.

cards.title: How did object detection work before YOLO?
body: Toggle to compare the old multi-step process with YOLO's single prediction.
cards.offNote: The system checks hundreds of regions, running a classifier on each. This is slow and complex.
cards.onNote: YOLO predicts all boxes and classes in one go, making detection much faster.

cards.title: YOLO's big leap: unified detection
body: YOLO treats object detection as a single regression problem. It predicts both where objects are and what they are directly from the image.
Instead of piecing together separate systems, YOLO's network sees the whole scene, learning to predict all objects and their locations in one step.

cards.title: Speed versus accuracy in YOLO
body: Slide to see how changing YOLO's speed affects its detection accuracy.
cards.low: Processes images extremely quickly but may miss small or overlapping objects.
cards.mid: Balances speed and accuracy, suitable for most real-time uses.
cards.high: Detects more accurately but takes longer, still much faster than earlier methods.

cards.title: What goes into YOLO?
body: Select the ingredients that make YOLO possible.

choices.label: Grid splitting
choices.note: Divides the image into cells to localize objects.

choices.label: Single neural network
choices.note: Processes the whole image in one pass.

choices.label: Bounding box regression
choices.note: Predicts precise coordinates for each object.

choices.label: Class probability prediction
choices.note: Estimates which object class is present.

choices.label: End-to-end training
choices.note: Optimizes the system as a whole for detection.

choices.label: Unified loss function
choices.note: Balances localization and classification errors during learning.

cards.title: YOLO's impact: speed and generalization
body: YOLO made real-time object detection practical, running at 45 frames per second or more. It also generalized well to new types of images.
While it sometimes makes localization errors, YOLO produces far fewer false positives and adapts to art or sketches better than older methods.

bars.label: Generalization
bars.note: Handles art and unusual images well

cards.caption: YOLO set a new standard for combining speed, accuracy, and flexibility in object detection.

cards.title: Why YOLO is a classic
body: YOLO's single-shot approach inspired a new wave of fast, practical object detectors for everything from self-driving cars to mobile phones.
Its influence endures because it shows how rethinking a problem can unlock both speed and simplicity, without sacrificing too much accuracy.

recap.label: Divide
recap.note: Split the image into a grid of cells.

recap.label: Predict
recap.note: Use a neural network to predict boxes and classes for each cell.

recap.label: Unify
recap.note: Train the whole system to optimize detection directly.

recap.label: Detect
recap.note: Output all objects in one fast pass.

cards.title: You Only Look Once: Unified, Real-Time Object Detection
body: Read the original paper to see how YOLO changed the object detection landscape.
